subject: Joint Distributions
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be random variables with joint pdf $f_{XY}(x, y) = \begin{cases} \frac{1}{4} & -1 \le x, y \le 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$ Determine (a) $\Pr(X^2 + Y^2 < 1)$, (b) $\Pr(2X - Y > 0)$, (c) $\Pr(|X + Y| < 2)$.
explanation: (a) $\Pr(X^2 + Y^2 < 1) = \int_0^{2\pi} \int_0^1 \frac{1}{4} r\,dr\,d\theta = \frac{\pi}{4}$. (b) $\Pr(2X - Y > 0) = \int_{-1}^1 \int_y^1 \frac{1}{4}\,dx\,dy = \frac{1}{2}$. Note that $\Pr(2X - Y > 0)$ is the area of the region bounded by the lines $y = 2x, x = -1, x = 1, y = -1$ and $y = 1$. A graph of this region will help you understand the integration process used above. (c) Since the square with vertices $(1, 1), (1, -1), (-1, 1), (-1, -1)$ is completely contained in the region $-2 < x + y < 2$, we have $\Pr(|X + Y| < 2) = 1$. Remark 39.1 Joint pdfs and joint cdfs for three or more random variables are obtained as straightforward generalizations of the above definitions and conditions.
